text: Before spending anything, check open prefixes vs suffixes (the digest shows them). Wanting a bench life craft with no open prefix is the classic wasted-currency mistake.
Every rare worn while leveling wants life plus a resist. If the item already has two of the three, bench the missing one instead of rerolling the item.
For a specific mod, essences beat chaos/alch spam: same full reroll, one mod guaranteed. At league start essences are plentiful and chaos are precious.
Decide the stop condition before the first orb ('T3+ life and any resist' beats 'perfect'). Leveling gear only has to carry the character ~10 levels.
Buy build-enablers (mandatory uniques, links); craft commodity rares (life/resist fillers, boots, weapons). On league days 1-2 trade is thin and overpriced, so crafting wins more often than usual.
Damage comes from the weapon: re-craft or re-recipe the weapon every few acts before investing in armour slots. Casters can ride the +1 gems vendor recipe for the whole campaign.
Item level caps mod tiers and the base's tags decide what can roll at all — the digest's pool is exact. Currency into the right white base beats currency into the wrong rare.
A strong 2-mod magic item beats a bad rare while leveling. Don't regal a good blue unless there's a plan (and an open affix) to finish it at the bench.
League-start scarcity order: transmutes, alts, augs and low essences are spendable; regals, alchs, scours and chaos are savings. Plan crafts around the cheap half.
Leveling uniques usually beat anything craftable in their slot. Spend the crafting budget on the slots the uniques don't cover.
Fractured mods survive every reroll. A fractured base with a build mod is the best craft target of the campaign — never vendor one unchecked.
